title: archidendron microcarpum
intro: Archidendron microcarpum, also known as the 'Mungkang' or 'Malaysian rattan', is a type of climbing palm tree native to Southeast Asia. This plant is rich in medicinal properties, including its leaves, seeds, and roots, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The leaves of Archidendron microcarpum have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering several health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and supporting digestive health. Traditionally, the plant's roots and seeds are used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are believed to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties.
